      "questions": [

          "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "Why did Einstein's teachers initially believe he would never succeed?",
          "options": [
            "A. He was too slow in learning mathematics.",
            "B. He spoke very late and disliked the rigid school discipline.",
            "C. He refused to play with other children during breaks.",
            "D. He had a physical disability that prevented him from writing."

          "sourceEvidence": [

          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "vsa",

    "evaluation": {
      "answerKey": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q1",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "B. He spoke very late and disliked the rigid school discipline.",
            "explanation": "The text states Einstein was a late talker and clashed with the regimented nature of German schools, leading teachers to doubt his success.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q2",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "B. Mechanical toys and solitary thinking.",
            "explanation": "Einstein preferred mechanical toys over social interaction, which fostered his independent thought processes.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q3",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "A. A strict system of discipline and order.",
            "explanation": "'Regimentation' refers to the imposition of strict rules that suppressed individuality, which Einstein hated.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q4",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "His independence/non-conformity or his deep love for mathematics/physics.",
            "explanation": "Any valid trait mentioned in the text such as 'independence', 'non-conformist nature', or 'deep-seated love for math' is acceptable.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "vsa"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q5",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "He felt stifled and left the school.",
            "explanation": "Einstein found the regimentation suffocating, leading him to leave Munich because he could not conform.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "vsa"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q6",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "To warn about the potential of an atomic bomb.",
            "explanation": "Einstein wrote to Roosevelt to alert them that Germany might develop nuclear weapons, though it inadvertently started the Manhattan Project.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "vsa"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q7",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "Einstein's refusal to follow rigid rules and his questioning nature made him a 'failure' in the traditional school system. He felt suffocated by rote learning, which led him to leave Munich.",
            "explanation": "Students must mention: 1) His non-conformist attitude/refusal to obey blindly (1 mark). 2) The result was conflict with teachers or leaving school because he felt stifled/suffocated (1 mark).",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "sa"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q8",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "The 'beautiful mind' refers not just to his high IQ, but to his humanitarian spirit. It signifies a combination of immense scientific logic and deep empathy for humanity.",
            "explanation": "Students must mention: 1) The title does not refer only to intelligence/IQ (1 mark). 2) It includes his concern for peace/humanitarianism/empathy (1 mark).",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "sa"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "Einstein was a non-conformist who disliked rigid systems. He spoke late and preferred solitary thinking with mechanical toys, which made him seem like a failure in school. However, this independence allowed him to question established laws of physics later in life.",
            "explanation": "Marks allocation: 1 mark for identifying his childhood traits (late talker, non-conformist). 2 marks for explaining how these traits led to success (independence/questioning nature vs rote learning). 2 marks for a coherent connection between the two points. Acceptable variations include mentioning his love for math/physics as a sanctuary.",
            "marks": 5,
            "subtype": "la"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q10",
            "sectionId": "section-prescribed-books",
            "sectionTitle": "Prescribed Books",
            "answer": "The author calls Einstein's mind 'beautiful' because of his humanitarianism. While he was a scientific genius (Relativity, E=mc²), he realized science could be destructive (atomic bomb). He transitioned from a physicist to a world citizen who campaigned for peace and a world government.",
            "explanation": "Marks allocation: 1 mark defining 'beautiful mind' as humanitarianism/empathy. 2 marks discussing his scientific achievements briefly. 2 marks explaining the shift to advocacy (peace, disarmament) after realizing the destructive power of science.",
            "marks": 5,
            "subtype": "la"
          }
        ]
      },
      "markingScheme": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q1",
            "totalMarks": 1,

              {
                "criterion": "Defines 'beautiful mind' as humanitarianism/empathy.",

            ]

      "rubrics": {
        "items": [

            "questionId": "q7",

          {

            "criteria": [

                "criterion": "Clarifies that 'beautiful' refers to humanitarianism/empathy, not just IQ.",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {

                "marks": 1

            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "The 'beautiful mind' refers to his humanitarian spirit. It is not just about being smart, but having a heart that cares for peace."
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "totalMarks": 5,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "Identifies key character traits (non-conformist, independent).",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Explains the conflict with school/rigid systems.",
                "marks": 2
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Connects these traits to his later success (questioning laws, independence).",
                "marks": 2
              }
            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "Einstein was a non-conformist who disliked rigid systems. He felt stifled by rote learning and left school. However, this independence allowed him to question established physics laws later in life."
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q10",
            "totalMarks": 5,
